meta.name: FS Flourishing Scale
meta.description: The FS is an 8-item self-report measure of flourishing, assessing important aspects of human functioning including relationships, self-esteem, purpose, and optimism.
meta.author: Diener et al. (2010)
meta.reference: Diener E, Wirtz D, Tov W, et al. New well-being measures: Short scales to assess flourishing and positive and negative feelings. Soc Indic Res. 2010;97(2):143-156.
meta.instruction: Below are statements about yourself. Please indicate how much you agree or disagree with each.

questions.text: I lead a purposeful and meaningful life
My social relationships are supportive and rewarding
I am engaged and interested in my daily activities
I actively contribute to the happiness and well-being of others
I am competent and capable in the activities that are important to me
I am a good person and live a good life
I am optimistic about my future

ranges.label: Low Flourishing
ranges.description: Your psychological flourishing is low. This indicates that your current sense of well-being and life satisfaction is relatively low. Focusing on self-care and lifestyle adjustments may be beneficial.
suggestions: Consider consulting a mental health professional
Try doing one fulfilling activity each day
Stay connected with friends and family to build a sense of belonging

ranges.label: Moderate Flourishing
ranges.description: Your flourishing is moderate with room for improvement. This level reflects a generally positive psychological state, where you can experience meaning and satisfaction in most aspects of life.
suggestions: Share your experiences and positive feelings with others
Helping others is also an effective way to enhance well-being

ranges.label: High Flourishing
ranges.description: Your psychological flourishing is high. Your well-being and positive experiences are at a high level, reflecting strong psychological health and life adaptation.
suggestions: Keep up the habits and activities that bring you happiness
Share your positive experiences with others
